mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an approaching recession, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 145000.0 in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and falling housing starts indicates a consumption-driven economy overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets expect, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive correlation with 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of rolling 3-year windows, or the combined signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100bps annually.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to stable demand conditions triggers supply-side inflation expectations, forcing higher real interest rates that discount equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.5 points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: Forward returns following the trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 3-year rolling horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations where GDP growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the prevailing inflation rate by more than 150 basis points, real borrowing costs become restrictive enough to suppress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL annualized rate exceeds 1.5
failure_condition: The real rate spread signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered periods, or the forward return distribution shows no statistically significant skew toward negative values.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising unemployment and stagnant payroll growth signals a labor market inflection point, causing institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined unemployment-payroll signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects strong consumer confidence and credit availability, which boosts construction-related corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing starts threshold fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 2-year rolling window.
